# fly_network_policy (Resource)

Manages a Fly.io network policy. Import using app_name/policy_id.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "fly_network_policy" "allow_web" {
  app    = fly_app.example.name
  name   = "allow-web-traffic"
  action = "allow"

  source {
    app = "frontend-app"
  }

  dest {
    app   = fly_app.example.name
    ports = "8080"
    proto = "tcp"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `app` (String) The name of the application. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `name` (String) The name of the network policy.

### Optional

- `rule` (Block List) Traffic rules for the policy. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rule))
- `selector` (Block, Optional) Selector to match which Machines the policy applies to. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--selector))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the network policy.

<a id="nestedblock--rule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rule`

Required:

- `action` (String) The rule action: 'allow'.
- `direction` (String) Traffic direction: 'ingress' or 'egress'.

Optional:

- `port` (Block List) Port and protocol specifications. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--rule--port))

<a id="nestedblock--rule--port"></a>
### Nested Schema for `rule.port`

Required:

- `port` (Number) Port number.
- `protocol` (String) Protocol: 'tcp' or 'udp'.



<a id="nestedblock--selector"></a>
### Nested Schema for `selector`

Optional:

- `all` (Boolean) Apply to all Machines in the app.
- `metadata` (Map of String) Match Machines by metadata key-value pairs.
